text: Since 1988, through numerous field investigations and in-depth studies, a large-scale underwater landslide system has been discovered and confirmed to exist at the Yellow River Estuary. Based on sonar images and shallow stratum wound records, landslides are classified, named and characterized. Research shows that there are three types of landslides in this landslide system: bottleneck-shaped landslides, translational plate-shaped landslides and rotational landslides. Many individual landslides converge in the downhill direction to form a compound landslide system, and sometimes dozens of landslides converge to form a large-scale and complex system stretching for several kilometers. Landslide gullies are widely distributed, cutting the surface layer of the bottom slope into fragmented pieces. Compared with the Mississippi River estuary, the underwater landslides in the Yellow River Estuary have the characteristics of high water content, frequent occurrence on the shallow surface, diverse micro-sizes, clear transportation mechanisms, all landslides being negative terrain, and wide distribution. They play an important role in the formation of the characteristics and development and evolution of the Yellow River estuary and the underwater delta. Different rheological combinations exist at each stage of landslide sediments, forming a complete flow spectrum. The transport of sediment from the estuarine bottom to the ocean is intermittent and eventful.

By introducing the slider motion term into the shallow water equation to consider the generation process of landslide surge waves, the extended shallow water equation was numerically solved using the fourth-order predictive correction scheme and the high-order finite difference scheme, and a finite difference model of landslide surge waves based on the shallow water equation was established. The existing experimental results were compared and verified with the numerical results of the established model. Based on this, numerical calculation and analysis were conducted on the movement of flat-bottomed underwater landslides, as well as the wave head characteristics generated under different slider lengths, maximum thicknesses, and movement speeds. The results show that the simulation results of the model in this paper are in good agreement with the experimental results, and the determination coefficient values all reach above 0.89, indicating that the model in this paper has high accuracy. The main influencing factor of the surge wave length is the slider length. As the slider length increases, the surge wave length also increases, while the slider movement speed and slider thickness have no significant effect on the generated surge wave length. The wave height of the surge is mainly affected by the movement speed of the slider (slider Fr) and the thickness of the slider. As the thickness of the slider increases, the wave height also increases. When the slider Fr is less than 1, the height of the first wave increases with the increase of the slider Fr. However, when the slider Fr is greater than 1, the height of the first wave decreases with the increase of the slider Fr, and the height of the first wave reaches its maximum value near when the slider Fr equals 1. The deformation of the inrush wave surface is mainly affected by the thickness of the slider and the slider speed. The thicker the slider and the closer the slider Fr is to 1, the more obvious the deformation of the inrush wave surface will be.

Underwater landslides are one of the common geological disasters. To solve the problem that discrete element PFC is difficult to simulate the underwater fluid environment, a computational method coupling computational fluid dynamics OpenFOAM and discrete element PFC is proposed. Aiming at the problem of scale similarity in fluid-structure coupling, a similarity method suitable for this coupling method was proposed and its feasibility was verified. The dynamic characteristics and accumulation morphology of underwater landslides based on this coupling method were analyzed through typical cases, and compared with the results of unidirectional coupled underwater landslides and onshore landslides. The results show that this coupling method can simulate the movement law of underwater landslides well, mainly manifested as a large thickness at the front end of the landslide body and an elliptical surface. The movement process and accumulation morphology of underwater landslides are quite different from those of onshore landslides. Compared with the unidirectional coupling method, the OpenFOAM-PFC bidirectional coupling has superiority.

According to the natural environmental characteristics of the sea area, the eastern coastal waters of Zhejiang can be divided into the following four sea areas: the slope area at the front edge of the modern Yangtze River underwater delta, the estuary and harbor area, the island group area, and the underwater shore slope area. Among them, the island group area is further divided into three sub-areas based on the development degree of the tidal channel: 1. The Northern area of Zhoushan Islands, 2. The Southern Area of Zhoushan Islands, and 3. The Southern Island Group Area. The research results show that soil instability is mainly distributed in the tidal channels of Zone 2 and Zone 2, and is most commonly found in the deep groove slopes of the channels, the concave banks of the channels, the intersections of the channels, and the edges of the residual highlands in the channels. Through the analysis of the distribution pattern of underwater landslides and the erosion and siltation characteristics of tidal channels, it is concluded that the increase in slope caused by the erosion and siltation changes of tidal channels is the main inducing factor of landslides.

The movement of submarine landslides may trigger tsunamis, damage offshore facilities and threaten the safety of the coastal zone. Research on tsunamis caused by submarine landslides at home and abroad is in its infancy. The Mih particle flow model was adopted to control the movement of sandy soil landslides with weak cohesion. The two-phase flow model was used to calculate the interaction between rock and soil mass and water body, and the RNG turbulence model was used to control the movement of water body. A fully coupled numerical analysis method for submarine landslides and tsunamis based on the particle flow model was constructed. The entire process of submarine landslide tsunamis was studied through a simple underwater landslide case in a water tank. Numerical analysis has reproduced typical submarine landslide and tsunami phenomena such as the non-uniform motion of the deformed sliding body, density differentiation flow, water sliding mechanism, and surge waves characterized by troughs, which indicates the validity of the numerical model. Weak cohesive and non-cohesive underwater landslides exist in many sea areas (including the northern part of the South China Sea). This numerical method is worthy of promotion and further research and improvement.

Based on the analysis of the regional environment of underwater landslides in the tidal channel area, the classification, structural characteristics, development scale and formation era of underwater landslides were studied. The results show that the landslides in this area can be classified into two types based on their morphological characteristics: integral landslides and fragmented landslides, with integral landslides having an absolute advantage. Most landslides have the characteristics of curved sliding surfaces and multi-level nature, and the vast majority of landslides occur in Holocene. Compared with other areas of the continental slope and continental shelf, due to the limitation of the passage space, the development scale of landslides in the study area is relatively small and has similar characteristics to land-based landslides.

Catastrophic tsunamis are sometimes caused by submarine landslide movements. The study of the inrush generation process of submarine landslides is the key to tsunami research. The formation of tsunamis is mainly controlled by factors such as the geometric dimensions of the sliding body, sliding speed, water depth, and sliding Angle. Based on these factors, the article designed and implemented orthogonal physical experiments of underwater rigid sliders. In the test, the thick-to-length ratio of the slider was between 0.035 and 0.180, the inclination Angle of the slope slide was between 10° and 16°, and the Froude number was between 0.18 and 0.70. Experimental analysis shows that the initial surge forms earlier than the slider stops. The initial trough (the maximum trough) is generated when sliding at the maximum speed, and the position of the trough is also the centroid position of the slider at the maximum speed. The typical characteristics of the initial surge wave are large troughs and small crests. Based on the input and output data of the experiment, the calculation formulas for the maximum trough and maximum peak were formed through regression derivation. The results of the experimental analysis have deepened the understanding of tsunamis generated by submarine landslides, providing a foundation and technical support for the prediction of tsunamis caused by submarine landslides.

Based on the identification of various survey methods, it was found that the contour lines of the seabed topography near Xiamen Hujiu Wharf were significantly distorted. The outer boulders and silt on the south side of the wharf are mutually flipped over. Honeycomb-like pores can be seen in the silt behind the sliding body. The area around the North Pier is discontinuously distributed with newly deposited grayish-yellow silt along the coast. In addition, the seismic images also show relatively severe diffraction waves and other phenomena. This paper conducts a comprehensive analysis of the sliding mechanism in combination with factors such as terrain, strata, and Marine hydrodynamic conditions. Without considering the effects of waves and earthquakes, different stability evaluations were conducted on the underwater slope body by using the limit equilibrium theory method and the arc strip division method for the rear wall slope. The research concludes that the formation of this submarine landslide is the combined result of natural geology, human activities, gravity, ocean dynamics, and stratigraphic factors.

This paper, in response to the application demands for the prevention and control of coastal landslide disasters in the nearshore project of Zhujiajian Island, takes the muddy viscous sediment seabed as the main research object, and based on the theories of rheology measurement, non-Newtonian fluid mechanics and sediment motion mechanics, comprehensively adopts research methods such as rheological test measurement, theoretical analysis, model tests and numerical simulation to conduct in-depth and systematic research on viscous seabed sediments Fundamental research on flow characteristics and rheological behavior testing, rheological model construction, underwater landslide model experiments under wave action, simulation of the disaster process of coastal landslide disasters, and acquisition of sliding accumulation features. The rheological properties of non-Newtonian fluids of seabed sediments, the multi-information evolution law of underwater landslide processes, and the sliding accumulation characteristics of seabed landslide disasters were analyzed emphatically. The disaster mechanism and evolution process of nearshore seabed landslides were revealed, providing an effective way for the effective and active prevention and control of seabed landslide disasters on Zhujiajian Island. The specific work and achievements are as follows :(1) Through case studies, the causes of typical submarine landslide disasters at home and abroad have been summarized: Internal factors - the material properties and geological characteristics of the slope body. The weakening of the strength characteristics of the slope body is a prerequisite for the occurrence of underwater landslides; External triggering conditions - When a landslide occurs, it is inevitably accompanied by a sudden change in external conditions, which is a necessary condition for the formation of submarine landslides. Based on the comprehensive analysis of the landslide dynamics process, the entire disaster evolution process of typical submarine landslide disasters has been generalized. The disaster process of submarine landslides is a multi-stage and multi-phase interaction process. Through literature collection and geological exploration data analysis, the disaster types of the Zhujiajian Island submarine landslide were defined, and the disaster stages included in the disaster evolution process of the Zhujiajian Island submarine landslide were identified, laying a foundation for the subsequent work.

The sea area of Zhoushan Archipelago is crisscrossed with gorges, and the phenomenon of slope instability in the gorges is generally developed, with varying scales. Frequent underwater landslides pose a significant threat to coastal engineering facilities such as submarine optical cables and port terminals. In December 2012, a shallow stratigraphic profilometer was used to explore the surrounding gorge area of Liuheng Island. Three major acoustic reflection interfaces and four stratigraphic units were identified. The upper three strata are Holocene sedimentary layers. Six underwater landslides were discovered, distributed along the slopes of Liuheng Island and the adjacent tidal deep trough slopes. The landslide structures are all integral landslides, and some have multi-level characteristics. In terms of the scale of the landslide mass, the volume of the landslide mass ranges from 1.6×10 ⁻ ⁵ to 2.7×10 ⁻ ⁷ m ³, among which there are 2 super-large landslides, 3 large landslides and 1 medium-sized landslide. Based on the correlation between the interface and the landslide mass, it is speculated that all underwater landslides occurred in the late Holocene since 2.5ka ago.

Landslide is an important disease of embankment engineering, and its monitoring methods have always been a research hotspot at home and abroad. Due to the fact that underwater landslides cannot be monitored by conventional methods such as remote sensing and daily patrols, they are often only discovered after the underwater foundation collapses and the embankment becomes unstable, which seriously affects flood control safety. This paper discusses an underwater landslide monitoring method based on underwater fine detection technology. By using the shipborne multi-beam sounding system and the optical imaging system of the unmanned remotely Operated Vehicle (ROV) for detection, information such as the range of underwater landslides and the size of landslide blocks is obtained. The results of applying this method to the underwater landslide monitoring work of a certain river channel show that it can efficiently and truly reflect the actual situation underwater, and has a good application prospect in the daily monitoring and repair and improvement work of slope protection.

Submarine landslide tsunamis, as the second most important form of tsunami after earthquake tsunamis, often occur in areas such as continental shelves and slopes near the coast. Due to their significant impact of nearshore disasters, complex physical mechanisms, and difficulty in monitoring and early warning, they cause huge losses of life and property to residents near the coast. Experimental research on waterborne landslide tsunamis is relatively thorough, while studies on underwater landslide tsunamis and partially submerged landslide tsunamis are relatively scarce. This study conducted a mechanism experiment on the induction of tsunamis by underwater landslides of scattered bodies on slopes in a large wave water trough. The sliding distance of the landslide body, the formation process of the tsunami wave, as well as the amplitude, wave velocity, and nonlinearity of the tsunami wave were investigated. The influence laws of the mass of the landslide body, water depth, and landslide Angle on the height of the tsunami wave caused by the landslide were systematically understood. And an improved empirical formula for the characteristic parameters of underwater landslide tsunamis was proposed.

In the past decade or so, with the significant progress of deep-sea geophysical exploration technology and the continuous exploration and development of deepwater oil and gas, the study of submarine landslides at the continental margin has become one of the hotspots in international deepwater research. In the deep water areas along the margins of most continents around the world, landslides are one of the most important processes for the transportation of sediments from the shelf to deep-sea basins. In slope and deep-water environments, the proportion of sediments related to landslides can even reach over 70%, and in some slope deep-water sediments, they are even composed entirely of landslide bodies. Submarine landslide bodies, especially sandy landslide bodies, can form large-scale and stably distributed reservoirs in deep-sea basins. Currently, they have become a new highlight in deep-sea oil and gas exploration and development as well as in the growth of oil and gas reserves. Compared with the continental margin, whether underwater landslides develop in continental basins and the dynamic mechanisms, petrological characteristics, identification markers and oil and gas exploration value of their formation are all topics worthy of in-depth study. At present, Chinese scholars have discovered large-scale sliding rocks, sliding rocks and sandy clastic flows and other block transport deposits in the Songliao Basin, the Ordos Basin, the Bohai Bay Basin and the Pearl River Estuary Basin, indicating that landslide deposits may have developed in the Miocene lakes in China. However, the research on the seismic response, sedimentary petrological characteristics and exploration value of the landslide mass is extremely weak. In view of this, the author conducted an in-depth study on the seismic response, sedimentary characteristics, fracture formation mechanism and oil and gas exploration value of continental lake basin landslide bodies by using the core, logging, seismic and analytical laboratory data of two different types of basins, namely the depression period of the Songliao Basin and the fault depression period of the Jiuquan Basin. Research shows that continental lake basin landslide bodies have distinct landslide morphologies and sedimentary structures. Seismically, they exhibit hilly shapes, transparent and disheveled reflective features. Tensile fracture structures are developed at the head, and thrust structures and small folds can be seen at the toe. Concentric circular pressure ridges are generally present on the plane. The lithology of sliders is diverse, mainly including conglomerate, sandstone or mud shale. In addition to the common massive sandstone, rotating fragments and landslide breccia, there are also a large number of sedimentary deformation or soft sedimentary deformation structures developed on the core. Large-scale underwater landslide bodies are generally developed in continental lake basins, with areas ranging from 4 to 300 square kilometers, and the maximum thickness of the landslide bodies can reach 150 meters. The sandy landslide mass at the leading edge of the delta can not only form conventional oil and gas reservoirs in the center of the lake basin, but also develop a large number of non-structural fractures inside the landslide mass due to tension and compression during the sliding process. Therefore, it is also very conducive to the formation of fractured oil and gas reservoirs in the deep layer. The research results have significant scientific significance for expanding the exploration fields of continental basins in our country.

Accurate prediction of the movement of landslide bodies and the generation of free surface waves is the key to the study of underwater landslides. In this paper, by combining the respective advantages of smoothed Particle hydrodynamics (SPH) and Discrete Element Method (DEM), and considering the infiltration effect of water bodies in granular landslide, an SPH-DEM coupling model suitable for analyzing the inundation problem of underwater granular landslide is established. Through the infiltration example of the porous medium in the U-shaped tube, the accuracy of the infiltration force calculated by the model was further verified, and the feasibility and effectiveness of the SPH-DEM coupling model in simulating the soil-water coupling problem were proved. The underwater deformable landslide problem was simulated and calculated. The infiltration mechanism of underwater bulk landslide was expounded, and the influence of the infiltration coefficient on the deformation and wave shape of bulk landslide was systematically studied. It was found that with the increase of the infiltration coefficient, the deformation degree of bulk landslide and the maximum first wave height both change logarithmically. Further considering the influence of different soil masses and permeability coefficients on the morphology of granular landslide and the height of the surge, the fitting formula for the deformation of granular landslide is obtained.

To improve the deterioration of water quality in lakes and reservoirs caused by seasonal water temperature stratification, internal waves were artificially induced by disturbing underwater landslide bodies. The characteristics of internal waves induced by underwater landslide movement and their damage to the water temperature stratification structure were explored. The results show that: The destruction of the temperature stratification structure of water bodies by underwater landslides is mainly achieved through the exchange of upper and lower water bodies caused by disturbances. During this process, the effect of internal waves is persistent and relatively gentle. The inclination Angle of the landslide and the movement speed of the landslide mass have significant influences on the destruction effect of stratified water bodies. The internal wave scale induced by a landslide inclination of 45° is larger than that induced by a 60° inclination.

An improved ISPH multiphase flow was constructed for submarine landslides. The numerical model adopts the Herschel-Bulkley-Papanastasiou rheology. The model characterizes the non-Newtonian fluid properties of clay landslides through particles. The shift technique and gradient correction method improve the pressure stability of the model. And its accuracy was verified in the underwater landslide test simulation. It reveals the influence of the geometric features of the seabed topography on the slide-water coupling cooperation. Due to the influence of the slope, when the slope corner is 190°, the landslide mass shows the farthest. The motion characteristics, with a normalized sliding distance of 170° from the slope corner. The 220° working condition has been improved by 14%, which is beneficial for the layout of submarine pipelines and the site selection of platforms. Key terrain parameters are provided. Special attention should be paid to steep slopes in engineering design. The hydrodynamic lifting effect and water sliding effect caused by corners (>200°). Therefore, such terrain is prone to the formation of turbidity currents and the diffusion of particles, which may increase. Increase the risk of impact on submarine infrastructure. The presence and absence of water conditions have an impact on the morphology and kinematic characteristics of debris flows. There are significant differences. Environmental water passes through buoyancy - shear force - resistance - lubrication. The multiple coupling effects make the sliding body form a unique 'restricted acceleration - long' 'Two-stage dynamic characteristics of travel and sliding.' Particle-fluid momentum exchange effect. It maintained the continuous movement of the underwater mass and facilitated the super-long migration of the submarine landslide. The phenomenon provides a certain explanation. The ISPH multiphase flow model constructed in this study is on a small-scale seabed. It shows good applicability in the simulation of landslide movement and can predict no. Under the same terrain conditions, the migration and influence range of the landslide mass can be the seabed. We provide early warning services for nearshore engineering disasters such as pipeline layout and offshore platform site selection. Theoretical basis. In addition, this model can also be extended to evaluate landslide inducements. The influence of wave generation.

The collapse and landslide process of granular materials is of great significance both in the theory of particle flow and in practical applications (such as landslides, tsunamis, debris flows, etc.). Due to the limitations of actual conditions, a large amount of experimental data and morphological features need to be obtained in experiments to provide a basis for the early warning of real natural disasters. At present, there are relatively few experimental observation results related to the movement process of underwater granular materials. A series of experiments were conducted in a two-dimensional wave water trough to study the collapse and landslide process of two-dimensional underwater granular materials and the characteristics of the resulting tsunami waves. A designed inclined plane was installed in a large wave water tank to conduct underwater particle landslide experiments. The slope of the inclined plane can be adjusted to 30°, 45° and 60° to simulate the landslide process and the generation of tsunami waves at different slopes. The slope surface is covered with a layer of acrylic sheet, while the smooth and horizontal bottom surface of the wavy water trough is made of concrete. A high-speed camera was placed on the side of the wave water trough to capture the movement profile of the granular material, and 11 wave meters were used to measure the time series of the free surface height. To study the influence of particle diameter on tsunami waves, two differentially sized glass beads with a specific gravity of 2.5 were used, and the initial volume fraction of the particle phase was 0.55 in both cases. To study the influence of the proportion of glass beads with different diameters on the experimental results, some tests used granular materials composed of glass beads of different diameters. The movement distance and dynamic characteristics of the front end of granular materials, as well as several near-field features of the generated tsunami waves, were studied. The contour changes of granular materials, the wave generation process, the causes of the thick and blunt morphology of the front-end particles, and the characteristics of particle rolling motion were described. The influences of landslide volume, hydrostatic depth, particle diameter and landslide Angle on wave velocity, wave period and energy conversion rate were discussed. Some empirical formulas have been developed to describe the characteristics of underwater landslide tsunami waves and conduct analysis.

Landslide tsunamis, as an important type of tsunami, have caused serious losses of life and property to residents near the coastal zone due to their complex mechanism and difficulty in early warning. Research on the formation process of underwater landslide tsunamis and the characteristics of tsunami waves is relatively scarce compared to that on water landslide tsunamis. Relevant theoretical numerical studies still lack sufficient measured data as verification basis. This study conducted an experimental study on the generation of tsunami waves by underwater landslides in a large two-dimensional wave water tank. By using an adjustable-angle inclined plane device and a partition mechanism installed in the wave water tank to form the conditions of the landslide, two types of glass beads with different diameters were used as the bulk particles constituting the landslide body. A high-speed camera was employed to record the contour changes of the landslide body during the landslide process from the side. 1 A wave height meter records the time series of tsunami waves generated by underwater landslides and the propagation process of the tsunami waves. Firstly, the changes in the contour of the landslide mass during the landslide process were analyzed. It was noted that the thick and blunt shape of the front end of the landslide mass during the landslide process and its cause was analyzed, namely the curling phenomenon at the front end of the landslide mass. The experiment investigated the wave velocities of the first and second waves of the tsunami and found that the tsunami waves generated by underwater landslides had undergone dispersion. The wave velocity of the first wave peak was significantly greater than that of the second wave peak, and the two could be considered to remain basically unchanged during the propagation process. The wavelengths of the first wave and the second wave were investigated, and the corresponding empirical formulas were obtained. The results show that the relative height of the landslide mass and the landslide Angle are the main factors affecting the wavelength. The wavelength increases during the propagation process, which is consistent with the results of theoretical research. The wave energy conversion rates of the first and second waves were investigated, that is, the ratio of the wave energy recorded by the first wave height meter in the time series to the loss of mechanical energy of the landslide body during the landslide process. As the water depth of the purified water increases, the energy conversion rate of the first wave and the second wave decreases. For the first wave, as the landslide Angle increases, the energy conversion rate also increases. The energy conversion rate of tsunami waves generated by a landslide composed of particles of a single diameter is generally greater than that of a landslide composed of particles of different diameters.

For the problem of underwater bulk landslide sea whistling, the wave-dynamic characteristics of underwater sliding slope tsunamis under different relative mass, relative submergence depth, relative initial position and relative particle size of bulk were experimentally studied. The test results show that it was produced on the underwater loose slope one Among the surging waves of the sea, the first The amplitudes of the peaks of the two waves are always greater than those of the first wave, and they are the maximum values among all the peak amplitudes. In the near-field region, the first wave and the second wave are present one Definite non-linear effect; Based on the results of this practical test, the experience proposed by W such as Ta k a b a t a k e has been corrected For the common type, its applicable range is expanded to the situation where the initial position of the opposite slope is set to zero.

The South China Sea is one of the most active regions in the world in terms of typhoon, earthquake and volcanic activities, with strong erosion around the basin and a high rate of deep-sea sedimentation. Frequent submarine landslides - turbid currents occur, frequently damaging submarine infrastructure such as communication cables. In addition, large amounts of water induced by factors such as earthquakes. The landslide has triggered geological disasters such as tsunamis in the South China Sea. But we do not understand the occurrence process, driving mechanism and history of these events. There is still limited understanding. Generally, by using reflected seismic data, buried ancient landslide deposits can be identified from the strata. However, it is reflected. The vertical resolution limitation of seismic data often only enables the identification of event layers with a thickness of over 10 meters. Therefore, based on conventional methods, still. It is difficult to identify large-scale event sediments on the seabed and obtain the age of the events on a time scale of 100,000 years (or less). At the edge of the deep-sea basin. The core has continuous sedimentation and is relatively easy to date, while preserving both deep-sea background sedimentation and ancient landslide sedimentation. The period and year of event deposition. Age can be defined by the background deposits at the top and bottom of the event layer. With the help of rock cores, large-scale projects such as submarine landslides can be identified on a scale of tens of thousands of years. The occurrence period of event deposition and its age limit. Sedimentary sequences from the 0.8-0.4 Ma period in the northern South China Sea basin (such as IODP 367-U1499A) preserve the frequent occurrence of collapsible soft sedimentary deformations and turbid-current sedimentary assemblages (with a total thickness of approximately 50 m), which can be traced in the reflected seismic profile. However, reflected earthquakes. Due to vertical resolution limitations, it is still impossible to distinguish whether the strata in this section record a single large-scale submarine landslide event or an aggregated occurrence. Multiple medium to small-scale incidents. The answer to this question will directly affect the correct understanding of the event deposition process, stages and events. Reliable age limit. Through conducting a comparative study of event deposition, we found that: (1) Similar combinations of event deposition also occurred in. (2) The ages of the IODP 349-U1432C core 50 km away can be compared, both occurring at 0.8-0.4 Ma. (3) All are soft in thick layers. Sedimentary deformation is the main feature and has a high degree of similarity. (4) All start with a sandy turbidity current deposit layer of meter-level thickness, and the top year. The ages are approximately 417,000 and 415,000 years respectively. Subsequent high-resolution event sedimentology studies will reveal the northern part of the South China Sea during the 0.8-0.4 Ma period. The types, periods, ages and driving mechanisms of the main events that occurred.
